data.reasoningText:
Now let me build the project to see the actual error.

The issue is clear: The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output path, but it never adds that file to the `Compile` item group. So the file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means it runs before compilation, but the generated file needs to be added to the `@(Compile)` items for the compiler to pick it up.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item after writing the file.


Let me build it to confirm the fix works.

arguments.description: Build the project to see failure

data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file in compilation:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
As I suspected. The file is generated (we can see "Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs") but the compiler doesn't see it because it's never added to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` after the `WriteCodeFile` task in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` MSBuild target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. The compiler had no idea the file existed.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>` within the target, right after the file is written. This tells the C# compiler to include the generated source file during compilation.
